steps.title: Read the vertex from vertex form
steps.content: $f(x) = \frac{1}{7}(x-6)^2 + 4$ is in form $a(x-h)^2 + k$, so the vertex is at $(h, k) = (6, 4)$.

steps.title: Decide minimum or maximum
steps.content: Since $a = \frac{1}{7} > 0$, the parabola opens upward, so the vertex gives a minimum. The minimum height is $k = 4$ inches, occurring at $x = 6$ seconds (not at $x = 0$).

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• A) Minimum height was 4 inches: matches $k = 4$ with $a > 0$.
  • B) Minimum height was 6 inches: 6 is the $x$-coordinate (time), not the height.
  • C) Height was 4 inches when it started: at $x = 0$, $f(0) = \frac{36}{7} + 4 \approx 9.14$, not 4.
  • D) Height was 6 inches when it started: same issue, and 6 is the time of the minimum.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
